title: ArtifactExplorer Scenario frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter of a scenario file under data/scenarios/<name>.md. Scenarios describe a forensic convergence — an analyst walkthrough or pattern that links several artifacts through join-key concepts.

name.description: Human-readable scenario title. Used verbatim in the scenarios picker UI.

anchors.description: Exit-node anchors that frame the scenario. Minimum 2 total: one entry (investigation starting point) and at least one conclusion (attribution terminus). Every anchor must be an identifier-kind concept name or an artifact flagged exit-node.

severity.description: Intended-use tier for the scenario. reference = demonstrates a canonical pattern; playbook = step-by-step investigative walkthrough; case-study = narrative-driven incident reconstruction.

narrative.description: Longer framing prose — the 'when to use this chain' rationale.

artifacts.description: DEPRECATED — scheduled for sunset once every step populates `convergence:`. Today: UNION of artifacts across all steps (if stepwise) OR the overlay set. primary = must-have; corroborating = supplementary. Will be derived from each step's convergence.input-sources after authorship passes complete.

join-keys.description: DEPRECATED — scheduled for sunset. Derivable from the union of each step's convergence.join-chain once authorship is complete. Kept during migration as a narrative pointer to the concepts threading the scenario.

steps.description: Optional stepwise convergence-chain progression. Each step = one analyst question.

provenance.description: Source attribution. Each item is either a source-registry ID (string) or an object {source, section?, note?}. Every ID must resolve to an entry in schema/sources.yaml.

question.description: The investigative question this step answers.

artifacts.description: DEPRECATED — derivable from the referenced convergence's input-sources once `convergence:` is populated. Retained for authoring convenience during migration.

join-key.description: DEPRECATED — redundant with the referenced convergence's join-chain once `convergence:` is populated. Retained during migration.

convergence.description: Optional reference to a tier-2 convergence file under convergences/<name>.md. When present, this step is an instance of that convergence pattern.

casey.description: Casey C-level ceiling for this step (e.g., 'C3', 'C3–C4', 'C4 (with corroboration)').

primary-source.description: Single source ID (from schema/sources.yaml) identified during the walkthrough audit as the strongest single-source attribution for this step's claim. Parallels the exit-node + convergence-pivot audit fields.

attribution-sentence.description: Verbatim sentence extracted from primary-source during the walkthrough audit that justifies this step's reasoning in real-world forensic terms. Should make the step's forensic conclusion defensible without reading the full source. APA-style citation anchored at the sentence end preferred per convention.
